zh.lifeSummary: 當Sage Griffith Davis於1521年出生在威爾士安格萊波多爾根時，她的父親James Griffith Davis46歲，母親Maud Ferch Morgan Lloyd41歲。她於1545年與Sir John Meyrick在威爾士布洛可爾聖 戴維斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1554年享年33歲。

nameMeanings.name: Davis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: patronymic meaning ‘Dafydd's (son)’, equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd, the Welsh form of David . The spelling Davis is more typical in southwestern England northwards as far as Lancashire, where the frequency of the surname largely reflects Welsh migration, but may sometimes represent a native English surname based on Davy (compare Davies ). Davis (including in the sense 2 below) is the eighth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans.
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Gaelic Mac Daibhéid ‘son of David’; see McDevitt . Compare Davies .

History: John Davis or Davys (c. 1550–1605) was an English navigator who searched for the Northwest Passage. — By the 18th century there were numerous persons named Davis in America, including the jurist John Davis, born in 1761 in Plymouth, MA, and Henry Davis, a clergyman and college president, who was born in 1771 in East Hampton, NY. — Jefferson Davis, born in 1808 in KY, was president of the Confederate States of America from 1861 to 1865.

variants.name: David
meanings: Jewish, Welsh, Scottish, English, French, Breton, Portuguese, Czech, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Dávid), Slovak (mainly Dávid): from the Hebrew personal name David (in Hungarian and Slovak spelled Dávid), interpreted as ‘beloved’. The name has been perennially popular among Jews, in honor of the Biblical king of this name. His prominence, and the vivid narrative of his life contained in the First Book of Samuel, led to adoption of the name among Christians in the Middle Ages in various parts of Europe. In Britain, the popularity of this as a personal name was increased for two reasons. Firstly by virtue of its being the name of the patron saint of Wales who was abbot-bishop in the 6th century at what became known as Saint David's in Pembrokeshire. There are numerous dedications and placenames honouring the saint in south Wales, and it is no coincidence that the modern surname is heavily concentrated there, especially in Glamorgan. Secondly, the name was borne by two kings of Scotland (David I, reigning 1124–53, and David II, 1329–71). Its popularity in Russia is largely due to the fact that this was the ecclesiastical name adopted by Saint Gleb (died 1015), one of two sons of Prince Vladimir of Kiev who were martyred for their Christian zeal. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from some other languages, especially Assyrian/Chaldean Dawid and Arabic Daud (with variants, such as Daoud and Dawood ).

History: Guillaume David from France married Marie Armand in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Davies
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of Davis , a patronymic equivalent to Welsh ap Dafydd ‘Dafydd's son’. The name is especially numerous in south Wales. This form of the name probably shows the influence of Davy , the common English form of the personal name David in the late medieval and early modern periods (see Davey ).

variants.name: Daves
meanings: Welsh and English: probably a variant of   Davis   or   Davies  .
In some cases also German (Däves): from a shortened form of the personal name Matthäus (see Matthew ). This surname is very rare in Germany.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of German   Tewes  , a cognate of 2 above.

nameMeanings.name: Sage
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly U.S.: apparently from the name of the herb (Middle English sauge, via Old French from Latin salvia); compare Sorrel and Bay . In part it may also have been taken from the vocabulary word meaning ‘wise’ (from Latin sapius).

nameMeanings.name: Griffith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Gruffudd
meanings: From Old Welsh Grip(p)iud, from an unexplained first element + a second element meaning ‘lord, prince’. Gruffydd ap Llewelyn ( d. 1063 ) was one of the most able rulers of Wales in the Middle Ages, scoring notable victories over the English until he was eventually defeated by King Harold in 1063 .

en.lifeSummary: When Sage Griffith Davis was born in 1521, in Bodorgan, Anglesey, Wales, her father, James Griffith Davis, was 46 and her mother, Maud Ferch Morgan Lloyd, was 41. She married Sir John Meyrick in 1545, in St Davids, Pembrokeshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1554, at the age of 33.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sage Griffith Davis est née en 1521, à Bodorgan, Anglesey, Galles, son père, James Griffith Davis, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Maud Ferch Morgan Lloyd, avait 41 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Meyrick en 1545, à St Davids, Pembrokeshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1554, à l’âge de 33 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sage Griffith Davis(이)가 1521년 웨일스 앤글레시 보도르건에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Griffith Davis(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Ferch Morgan Lloyd(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1545년 웨일스 p브로케실르 스트 데이비드즈에서 Sir John Meyrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1554년 33세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sage Griffith Davis родилась в 1521 г. в местности бодорган, англеси, Уэльс, её отцу по имени James Griffith Davis было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Ferch Morgan Lloyd, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Meyrick в 1545 г. в местности ст дэвидс, пемброкешир, Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1554 г. в возрасте 33 лет.
